methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Pano AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Sage Geosystems
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $400M

private.name: Reverion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $310M

private.name: CarbonCapture Inc.

private.name: Cemvita Factory
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $120M

data.narrative:
Commonwealth Fusion Systems, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Bob Mumgaard, has raised $2.863B to date and carries a last post-money valuation of $5.0B+. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 5 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita Factory) alongside 1 public comparable, with an additional 8 private peers informing sector context.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields an implied valuation of $9.5B from the $5.0B base. Peer-median analysis across the 5 comparable companies—valued between $120M and $500M with a median of $310M—provides a lower-bound cross-check. The peer cohort exhibits material dispersion, with Pano AI and Sage Geosystems at the upper end ($500M and $400M) and Cemvita Factory at the floor ($120M).

The triangulated range spans $264M (lower bound), $4.9B (median), to $10.9B (upper bound). The current $5.0B valuation aligns with the median estimate. Key uncertainties include the methodological tension between stage-progression logic (which assumes Commonwealth's trajectory mirrors peers) and peer-median results (which suggest substantially lower valuations for same-stage companies), likely reflecting Commonwealth's differentiated capital intensity and technology risk profile relative to the broader peer universe.
